title: A CRISPR guide and its off-targets
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Cas9 only cuts where a 20-nt guide matches next to a PAM (for SpCas9, an NGG). This scans a sequence for every valid protospacer, then hunts the rest of the DNA for near-matches — the off-target sites (up to 3 mismatches here) that make or break a genome edit. Off-target prediction is the central safety question of CRISPR therapeutics: the ideal guide is the one whose 20-mer is unique. Swap the enzyme to Cas12a and the PAM rules — and the whole candidate list — change.
